skill.name: fulltext-fetcher
skill.description: Fetch and save the original HTML of scientific literature webpages when given a URL, DOI, or PubMed PMID (triggered when you need archival-grade page HTML for downstream parsing or review).
skill.summary: Fetch original HTML from scientific literature webpages by URL, DOI, or PubMed PMID for archival and downstream processing.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 76 static analysis findings. All 8 high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives: the codebase uses no cryptographic operations. All 49 external commands findings in SKILL.md are false positives: they match backtick syntax inside markdown code blocks showing documentation examples, not executable code. The critical heuristic dangerous combination finding is a false positive: os.getenv reads the FULLTEXT_ALLOW_ALL configuration flag, not credentials. The 4 system reconnaissance findings are false positives triggered by PMID example text. True positives: the skill uses Python urllib for HTTP requests (expected for a fulltext fetcher), reads one env var for domain override configuration, and references hardcoded URLs to scientific databases. The skill uses Python standard library only, enforces a domain whitelist, and scored 90/100 Production Ready in independent evaluation.

low_findings.title: Python HTTP Network Requests
low_findings.description: The skill uses Python urllib to make HTTP requests to external websites. This is expected and necessary for the skill purpose of fetching scientific literature HTML. Domain access is restricted to a whitelist by default.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of urllib.request usage for HTTP fetching which is the core purpose of this skill.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Read Access
low_findings.description: The skill reads the FULLTEXT_ALLOW_ALL environment variable to configure domain whitelist override. This is a non-credential configuration flag and does not expose secrets or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of os.getenv call reading a single configuration variable, not credentials or tokens.

content.user_title: Fetch Scientific Literature HTML by URL, DOI, or PMID
content.value_statement: Researchers often need to archive the original HTML of scientific papers for offline review or downstream parsing. This skill resolves DOIs and PubMed PMIDs to their landing pages and saves the raw HTML to local files using only Python standard library tools.
actual_capabilities: Fetch and save raw HTML from a direct URL to a local file
Resolve a DOI to its landing page and save the resulting HTML
Fetch a PubMed record by PMID and optionally resolve its PMC article link
Batch process multiple URLs, DOIs, and PMIDs in a single command
Enforce a domain whitelist with opt-in override for safer browsing
Handle network timeouts with automatic retry and exponential backoff
limitations: Cannot access paywalled or subscription-only content
Saves raw HTML only and does not extract or parse text content
Only reads the FULLTEXT_ALLOW_ALL environment variable for configuration
Default domain whitelist covers only PubMed, NCBI, and doi.org

use_cases.title: Archiving Research Papers for Offline Review
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to download and preserve the HTML of multiple scientific papers for review during a conference trip with limited internet access.

use_cases.title: Building a Literature Dataset
use_cases.description: A data scientist collects HTML snapshots of PubMed articles to build a corpus for natural language processing and text mining.

use_cases.title: Systematic Literature Review Support
use_cases.description: A medical librarian batch-fetches HTML from DOI and PMID lists to create an archive of source materials for a systematic review project.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch a Single Paper by DOI
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the HTML for DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2 and save it to the outputs folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user who wants to fetch a single known paper

prompt_templates.title: Batch Fetch Multiple Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch HTML for these papers: the DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2 and PMID 23273568. Save all files to the literature-review directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to archive multiple sources at once

prompt_templates.title: Fetch with Custom Output Directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch PMID 23273568 and save the HTML to my-papers directory with the allow-all flag enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs output in a specific location and trusts the target domains

prompt_templates.title: Fetch and Resolve PMC Full Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch PMID 23273568 and check if a PubMed Central full text version is available. If found, save the PMC article HTML instead of the PubMed abstract page.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user who wants full text when available via PMC

output_examples.input: Fetch PMID 23273568 with PMC full text resolution
output_examples.output: Fetching https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23273568/... Found PMC link: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3614012/ Fetching PMC full text... Saved: outputs/pmc_23273568.html (128 KB)

output_examples.input: Fetch URL https://example.org/paper and PMID 12345678 together
output_examples.output: Fetching https://example.org/paper... Saved: outputs/example.org_paper.html (32 KB) Fetching https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12345678/... Saved: outputs/pmid_12345678.html (18 KB)

best_practices: Verify that the output directory exists and all generated files are non-empty after each fetch session
Use distinct --out-dir values when running multiple fetch operations in parallel to avoid file name collisions
Prefer the default domain whitelist for routine work and only use --allow-all when you have verified the target domains
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to fetch content from websites that require authentication or violate terms of service
Do not rely on this skill for extracting structured text or metadata from HTML (it only saves raw HTML)
Do not fabricate DOIs, PMIDs, or URLs to test the script with fake identifiers

faq.question: What input types does this skill accept?
faq.answer: It accepts direct URLs, DOIs (Digital Object Identifiers), and PubMed PMIDs (PubMed identifiers).

faq.question: What domains can it fetch from by default?
faq.answer: By default it allows doi.org, pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, and pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov.

faq.question: How do I allow fetching from other domains?
faq.answer: Use the --allow-all flag or set the FULLTEXT_ALLOW_ALL environment variable to 1.

faq.question: Does this skill extract text content from the HTML?
faq.answer: No. It saves the raw HTML as-is. Text extraction requires a separate parsing step.

faq.question: Can it access paywalled scientific articles?
faq.answer: No. It only fetches publicly accessible webpages and does not handle authentication.

faq.question: What happens if a network request fails?
faq.answer: The script retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff before reporting the error.
